content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accept the default_model precedence regression amendment. Reject the final-review default amendment because the code already defaults unspecified final-review backends to Codex xhigh plus Claude Opus 4.6, and the cited run state is explained by an explicit live project override rather than a default-resolution bug.

## Votes

- `fr-6-0736c40d`: Accept
  This is a real correctness regression. EffectiveConfig::load now always seeds an explicit default implementer selection of Codex, default_backend_runtime_settings() adds Codex role_models for implementer and final_reviewer, and target_for_family() prefers role_models before base_backend.model/default_model. That means a workspace or project override such as default_backend = codex plus default_model = override-model no longer changes the implementer target or the first final-review reviewer when Codex is selected. A user-facing override path is being silently outranked by compiled defaults.
- `fr-6-013a5281`: Reject
  The cited symptom does not demonstrate a default-resolution defect. Source defaults already resolve unspecified final_review.backends to [codex, claude], and existing tests assert the resulting panel is Codex gpt-5.4-xhigh plus Claude claude-opus-4-6. The UTM run snapshot instead matches an explicit live project override: .git/ralph-burning-live/projects/utm/config.toml sets final_review.backends to codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh and codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh, and project config loading prefers that live project root when present. Changing default resolution on this basis would overwrite legitimate per-project configuration rather than fix the actual source of the run state.
